title: Nursery Rhyme. DX. Natural History.
content:
[A Dorsetshire version.]

        'Twas the twenty-ninth of May, 'twas a holiday,
        Four and twenty tailors set out to hunt a snail;
        The snail put forth his horns, and roared like a bull,
        Away ran the tailors, and catch the snail who wull.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. DX. Natural History."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
